title: Wales: Barmouth & Mawddach Estuary (Craig Wen)

excursions.label: Mawddach Bike Trail ride to Dolgellau

stops.original_wording: Mawddach Bike Trail - a disused railway
stops.excerpt: It was the Mawddach Bike Trail - a disused railway. We ran about 6 of the 16 miles.
We looked around Dolgellau & had a good lasagna lunch.

unresolved.issue: Probably Machynlleth (town in Wales); mentioned as the origin of a survey group at the campsite, not a place visited by the family — not a stop

boundary_flags: mixed_end: diary ends mid-sentence on B_0188_R; end date and return journey not recorded in this spread
summary: Simon and Eve (with their 6-month-old dog Tinder) made a short trip to Craig Wen campsite (LL39 1BQ) on the Mawddach Estuary near Barmouth, departing Birmingham on Friday 27 April 2012. During the stay they cycled part of the Mawddach Bike Trail (a disused railway) to Dolgellau and enjoyed spectacular estuary views; the diary ends mid-sentence so the return date is unknown.
extraction_notes: Only Simon, Eve and Tinder are mentioned as travellers — no children named. Craig Wen has a specific postcode (LL39 1BQ) which gives precise geocoding. Dolgellau appears both in the narrative (bike-trail day) and on a plum jam label (B_0188_R), confirming the visit. 'Mochant[leth]' is truncated at a page edge and most likely refers to Machynlleth; it denotes the origin of other campers, not a place the family visited. The diary entry on B_0188_R ends mid-sentence ('it was windy & rainy[so]'), indicating the spread boundary falls mid-trip; end date is therefore null.
